input.plan:
# Milestone 0 Implementation Plan — project-stay-in-touch

## Context

**What is this?** A Rails app for the Northwestern CS Software Studio class, "stay-in-touch" / Serendipity: a tool that helps people intentionally maintain friendships by tracking who they want to stay in touch with and how often, plus a log of actual catch-ups. The long-term vision (Python prototype in `claude-initial-files/`) uses Google Calendar + Forms + a daily scheduler to auto-pair friends. **Milestone 0 is not that.** M0 is the minimum viable Rails foundation on which future milestones layer features.

**Why now?** The repo currently contains only `initial-deliverable.txt` and the Python concept files. None of the M0 requirements are met: no Rails app, no models, no tests, no CI, no Heroku, no README/wiki. This plan produces a complete M0 submission.

**Outcome:** A Rails 7 app with two resources (Person, Event), full CRUD, Bootstrap UI, 20+ seeds, RSpec tests running in GitHub Actions, deployed to Heroku, documented in `README.md` and `wiki.md`.

**Team (GitHub):** `AMindToThink` (Matthew), `akurdia`, `josyao1`, `mjgaughan`. Repo: `NU-CS-Software-Studio-Spring-26/project-stay-in-touch`.

**Design principles (user-stated):** well-documented, well-structured, maintainable, extensible. Concretely: thin controllers, fat-but-focused models with validations, shared layout/partials, named routes via `resources`, RSpec model + request specs, YARD-style doc comments only where behavior isn't obvious from names, all domain logic discoverable from `app/models/` and `app/services/`.

---

## Tech Stack

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Ruby | 3.3.x (latest Heroku-supported patch) | Supported on Heroku-24 stack; matches class expectation of current Ruby. |
| Rails | 7.2.x | Stable, Heroku-ready, importmap + propshaft defaults work with Bootstrap via `bootstrap` gem. |
| DB (dev/test) | SQLite | Zero setup for teammates cloning the repo. |
| DB (prod) | PostgreSQL | Required on Heroku; configured only in `production` group. |
| Frontend CSS | `bootstrap` gem + `sassc-rails` | First-party SCSS; no CDN dependency; works offline and on Heroku. |
| JS bundling | importmap-rails (Rails default) | No Node build on Heroku, keeps buildpack simple. |
| Tests | RSpec (`rspec-rails`, `factory_bot_rails`, `shoulda-matchers`) | Matches the class's `hw2` pattern (workflow runs `bundle exec rspec`). |
| CI | GitHub Actions (`ruby/setup-ruby`) | Mirrors hw2's `ruby.yml` workflow so graders see familiar output. |
| Lint (optional, nice-to-have) | `rubocop-rails-omakase` | Rails 7.2 default; keeps style consistent across four contributors. |

---

## Data Model

Two primary domain models (`Person`, `Event`) joined many-to-many via an `EventParticipant` join model. The deliverable requires a minimum of 2 models and says associations are "welcome"; the join model is pure plumbing and keeps the design extensible (future fields: `role`, `rsvp_status`, `was_organizer`).

### `Person`
A person you want to stay in touch with.

| Field | Type | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| `name` | string | `presence: true` |
| `email` | string | `presence: true`, `uniqueness: true` (case-insensitive), format validation |
| `timezone` | string | `presence: true`, inclusion in `ActiveSupport::TimeZone.all.map(&:name)`, default `"America/Chicago"` |
| `preferred_start_hour` | integer | `0..23`, must be `<= preferred_end_hour` |
| `preferred_end_hour` | integer | `0..23` |
| `frequency_weeks` | decimal(5,2) | `> 0`, default `4.0` — how often you'd like to catch up |
| `notes` | text | optional |

```ruby
has_many :event_participants, dependent: :destroy
has_many :events, through: :event_participants
```

Helper methods for later reuse (kept tiny, documented):
- `#latest_event` → `events.order(occurred_at: :desc).first`
- `#days_until_due` → `frequency_weeks * 7 - days_since(latest_event&.occurred_at)` (returns `nil` if no events; negative when overdue). Exposed as a read-only field on the index so the app already feels purposeful even under the "Just the data + UI" M0 scope — the number is computed on the fly, no extra schema.

### `Event` (renamed from "Interaction" per user)
A logged catch-up — a call, coffee, group dinner, etc. Can involve one or many People.

| Field | Type | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| `occurred_at` | datetime | `presence: true`, `<= Time.current` |
| `medium` | string | `presence: true`, inclusion in `%w[call coffee text video in_person other]` |
| `title` | string | optional short label (e.g., "Birthday dinner") |
| `notes` | text | optional |

```ruby
has_many :event_participants, dependent: :destroy
has_many :people, through: :event_participants
validates :people, presence: true  # must have ≥1 participant
```

Scopes:
- `Event.recent` → `order(occurred_at: :desc)`

### `EventParticipant` (join model)
Pure join table; one row per (Person, Event) pair.

| Field | Type | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| `person_id` | bigint (FK) | `presence: true`, index, composite unique with `event_id` |
| `event_id` | bigint (FK) | `presence: true`, index, composite unique with `person_id` |

```ruby
belongs_to :person
belongs_to :event
validates :person_id, uniqueness: { scope: :event_id }
```

No UI is dedicated to `EventParticipant` — participants are managed via checkboxes on the Event form. It's intentionally not one of the "4 views" called out in the deliverable; the 4 views remain People index/show + Events index/show.

### Migrations (generated via `rails g model`)
- `db/migrate/<ts>_create_people.rb`
- `db/migrate/<ts>_create_events.rb` (no `person_id` FK — participants live in the join table)
- `db/migrate/<ts>_create_event_participants.rb` (`t.references :person`, `t.references :event`, `add_index ..., [:person_id, :event_id], unique: true`)

---

## Routes & Views

`config/routes.rb`:
```ruby
root "people#index"
resources :people
resources :events
```

This produces the 4 **required** views explicitly called out in the deliverable (index + show per model) plus the new/edit views needed for CRUD:

| Route | View | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `GET /people` | `people/index` | Table of all people with name, email, frequency, days-until-due, actions |
| `GET /people/:id` | `people/show` | Single person + their events timeline (via `person.events`) |
| `GET /events` | `events/index` | Table of all events, showing participants as comma-separated links |
| `GET /events/:id` | `events/show` | Single event detail with all participants listed as linked names |
| `new`/`edit`/`create`/`update`/`destroy` | standard Rails | CRUD completeness |

**Consistency across views** (deliverable requirement: "feel of one product"):
- Shared `app/views/layouts/application.html.erb` with a Bootstrap navbar linking **People**, **Events**, and **Add Person / Add Event**.
- Shared `_flash.html.erb` partial for notices/alerts.
- Shared `_form.html.erb` partials per resource. Event form uses a Bootstrap `.form-check` list of checkboxes (one per Person) bound to `event.person_ids` via a `people_ids: []` strong param.
- `app/views/shared/_breadcrumbs.html.erb` for show/edit pages.
- Every show page has "Back to index," "Edit," and "Delete" buttons wired consistently.

---

## Bootstrap UI

- Install via `bootstrap` gem (`~> 5.3`) + `sassc-rails`.
- Replace `app/assets/stylesheets/application.css` with `application.scss` that `@import "bootstrap"` and a small `custom.scss` partial for project-specific tweaks.
- Import Bootstrap JS bundle via importmap (`bin/importmap pin bootstrap @popperjs/core`).
- Required Bootstrap elements actually used (matching deliverable "at least table, buttons, form, the more the better"):
  - **table**: index pages use `.table .table-striped .table-hover`
  - **buttons**: all action buttons use `.btn .btn-primary` / `.btn-outline-danger` / etc.
  - **form**: new/edit use `.form-control`, `.form-label`, `.form-select`, `.form-text`
  - **navbar**: responsive `.navbar .navbar-expand-lg` in the layout
  - **card**: show pages wrap person/event details in `.card`
  - **badge**: show event medium as a colored `.badge`

---

## Seed Data

`db/seeds.rb` creates **12 people + 15 events + ~25 event_participants ≈ 52 rows total**, well above the 20-entry minimum (the deliverable's "entries" likely means user-visible domain rows — People + Events alone already hit 27).

Structure:
- 12 `Person` rows with varied timezones (Chicago, NYC, LA, London, Tokyo), preferred hours, frequencies (1–8 weeks), realistic notes.
- 15 `Event` rows spread across the past 3 months, across all media (`call`, `coffee`, `text`, `video`, `in_person`, `other`).
- Mix of solo events (1 participant) and group events (2–4 participants) so the many-to-many is exercised: e.g., "Birthday dinner" links 4 people; most calls link 1 person.
- Created via `Event.create!(..., people: [alice, bob])` so ActiveRecord inserts the join rows automatically.
- Script is idempotent: `EventParticipant.destroy_all; Event.destroy_all; Person.destroy_all` at the top (in that order to respect FK constraints) so `rails db:seed` can be re-run safely.
- Uses plain Ruby arrays of hashes — no Faker dependency — so output is stable/reviewable.

---

## Testing (RSpec)

Goals: cover validations, associations, and the key controller paths. Generated with AI assistance as the deliverable explicitly allows.

Structure (mirrors hw2 conventions):
```
spec/
├── rails_helper.rb
├── spec_helper.rb
├── models/
│   ├── person_spec.rb             # validations, associations, #days_until_due, #latest_event
│   ├── event_spec.rb              # validations, associations, scopes, "must have ≥1 person"
│   └── event_participant_spec.rb  # join-uniqueness, presence of both FKs
├── requests/
│   ├── people_spec.rb      # GET index/show/new/edit, POST create, PATCH update, DELETE destroy
│   └── events_spec.rb      # same, including create/update with people_ids: [...] param
└── factories/
    ├── people.rb
    ├── events.rb                  # factory builds events with 1 participant by default via transient
    └── event_participants.rb
```

- `shoulda-matchers` for compact validation/association specs.
- `factory_bot_rails` for test data.
- `DatabaseCleaner` not needed — use Rails transactional fixtures.
- Target: ~30 specs total, green locally via `bundle exec rspec`.

---

## Continuous Integration

File: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (modeled on hw2's `ruby.yml`).

```yaml
name: CI
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: ruby/setup-ruby@v1
        with:
          ruby-version: '3.3'
          bundler-cache: true
      - run: bin/rails db:prepare
      - run: bundle exec rspec
```

Badge in README links back to Actions runs. Branch protection is out of scope for M0 but can be added when team agrees.

---

## Heroku Deployment

- `Procfile`: `web: bundle exec rails server -p $PORT -e $RAILS_ENV` + `release: bundle exec rails db:migrate`.
- `config/database.yml` uses `ENV["DATABASE_URL"]` in production (Rails default already does this).
- Add `pg` gem to `:production` group, keep `sqlite3` in `:development, :test`.
- `bundle lock --add-platform x86_64-linux` so Heroku's Linux platform resolves gems.
- Provision: `heroku create stay-in-touch-<suffix>` (team picks suffix), add `heroku-postgresql:mini` add-on, push, `heroku run rails db:seed`.
- Stack: `heroku-24` (default for new apps in 2026).
- Document exact commands in README so teammates can redeploy.

---

## Documentation

### `README.md` (required)
Sections:
1. **MVP statement** — one paragraph from Serendipity product doc, trimmed: "A web app that helps you intentionally stay in touch by tracking the people who matter to you, how often you'd like to reach out, and your history with them."
2. **Team** — table of names + GitHub handles (`@AMindToThink`, `@akurdia`, `@josyao1`, `@mjgaughan`).
3. **Live app** — Heroku URL.
4. **Local development** — `bundle install`, `bin/rails db:setup`, `bin/rails server`.
5. **Tests** — `bundle exec rspec`.
6. **Deployment** — Heroku steps.
7. **Communication section (required verbatim by deliverable):**
   - Primary channel (TBD during kickoff — group chat platform)
   - Standup cadence
   - Decision rule (e.g., lazy consensus within 24 h, else majority vote)
   - How we split work (GitHub Issues assigned per feature)
   - Response-time expectation
8. **CI badge**.

### `wiki.md` (required)
Sections:
1. **Problem** — distilled from `claude-initial-files/serendipity-product-report.md`.
2. **Miro board link** — OO design (placeholder link for team to fill in).
3. **Object-oriented design overview** — class diagram described in text: Person ↔ Event association, future `User`, `Pair`, `ScheduledCall` classes sketched.
4. **Future features (post-M0)** — user auth, mutual-pair matching, Google Calendar integration, automatic scheduler (cron), notification emails, admin dashboard.
5. **Similar products** — Monaru, Dex, Clay, UpHabit, Nat.app — with one-line differentiators.
6. **References** — link back to the original Python prototype in `claude-initial-files/` for historical context.

### `.gitignore`
Use Rails's generated `.gitignore` + explicit entries for `.DS_Store`, `*.swp`, `.env*`, `/tmp/*`, `/log/*`, `/storage/*`, `/public/assets`, and `/coverage`.

---

## File / Directory Layout After Implementation

```
project-stay-in-touch/
├── .github/workflows/ci.yml         # NEW
├── .gitignore                       # NEW (Rails default + additions)
├── Gemfile / Gemfile.lock           # NEW
├── Procfile                         # NEW (Heroku)
├── README.md                        # NEW
├── wiki.md                          # NEW
├── Rakefile, config.ru, bin/, etc.  # Rails generated
├── app/
│   ├── controllers/
│   │   ├── application_controller.rb
│   │   ├── people_controller.rb
│   │   └── events_controller.rb
│   ├── models/
│   │   ├── person.rb                # with #days_until_due, validations
│   │   └── event.rb                 # with scopes, validations
│   ├── views/
│   │   ├── layouts/application.html.erb   # Bootstrap navbar + flash
│   │   ├── shared/_breadcrumbs.html.erb
│   │   ├── people/{index,show,new,edit,_form,_person}.html.erb
│   │   └── events/{index,show,new,edit,_form,_event}.html.erb
│   └── assets/stylesheets/application.scss  # @import "bootstrap"
├── config/
│   ├── routes.rb                    # root + two resources
│   ├── database.yml
│   └── importmap.rb                 # pins bootstrap, @popperjs/core
├── db/
│   ├── migrate/*_create_people.rb
│   ├── migrate/*_create_events.rb
│   ├── schema.rb
│   └── seeds.rb                     # 12 people + 15+ events
├── spec/
│   ├── rails_helper.rb
│   ├── spec_helper.rb
│   ├── models/{person,event}_spec.rb
│   ├── requests/{people,events}_spec.rb
│   └── factories/{people,events}.rb
└── claude-initial-files/            # PRESERVED (Python prototype + product doc)
    └── (unchanged — referenced by wiki.md)
```

`initial-deliverable.txt` can stay (useful reference) or be deleted — team's call.

---

## Implementation Order (recommended for teammate hand-off)

Keep each step individually committable so commits from all team members are easy to distribute.

1. `rails new . --database=sqlite3 --css=bootstrap --skip-bundle` in-place, then `bundle install`. Commit.
2. Add `.gitignore` additions, RSpec + FactoryBot + Shoulda, `rails g rspec:install`. Commit.
3. `rails g model Person ...`, `rails g model Event ...`, `rails g model EventParticipant person:references event:references`, add composite unique index in the migration, `db:migrate`, validations, model specs. Commit.
4. `rails g scaffold_controller` (or hand-written controllers) for People + Events; Event controller permits `people_ids: []`; request specs covering create/update with multiple participants. Commit.
5. Views: layout, navbar, Bootstrap index/show/new/edit, `_form` partials. Commit.
6. `db/seeds.rb` with 12 people + 15+ events; `rails db:seed`; manual smoke in browser. Commit.
7. `.github/workflows/ci.yml`; push and confirm green. Commit.
8. Heroku app create, `Procfile`, `pg` gem, `bundle lock --add-platform`, deploy, `heroku run rails db:seed`. Commit.
9. `README.md` + `wiki.md` with Heroku URL, CI badge, Communication section, MVP, team. Commit.
10. Distribute small follow-up commits across teammates to satisfy "commits from all team members" (e.g., assign each teammate a refinement — new seed data, CSS tweak, test addition, wiki section).

---

## Critical Files to Modify / Create

Most of these are **generated** by Rails — the plan is mainly about what the generators produce and what we fill in.

- `Gemfile` — add `bootstrap`, `sassc-rails`, `rspec-rails`, `factory_bot_rails`, `shoulda-matchers`, `pg` (:production), `sqlite3` (:dev/:test).
- `app/models/person.rb` — validations + `has_many :events, through: :event_participants` + `#days_until_due`, `#latest_event`.
- `app/models/event.rb` — validations (including ≥1 participant) + `has_many :people, through: :event_participants` + scopes.
- `app/models/event_participant.rb` — join with uniqueness validation.
- `app/controllers/people_controller.rb`, `events_controller.rb` — standard 7 REST actions with strong params; `events_controller` permits `people_ids: []`.
- `app/views/layouts/application.html.erb` — Bootstrap navbar, flash partial.
- `db/seeds.rb` — 12 + 15.
- `spec/models/*`, `spec/requests/*`, `spec/factories/*`.
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `.gitignore`, `Procfile`, `README.md`, `wiki.md`.

---

## Domain Logic Re-Used from the Python Prototype

Preserved as future hooks (not implemented in M0, but data model supports them):
- `frequency_weeks` on `Person` — directly maps to the "how often" concept in `scheduler.py`.
- `occurred_at` on `Event` — feeds future `last_calls` equivalent.
- `preferred_start_hour` / `preferred_end_hour` — future availability windowing.
- `timezone` — future Calendar integration.

In wiki.md's "Future features," we document that the geometric-mean pairing, random jitter, and Google Calendar lookup (from `claude-initial-files/scheduler.py` lines 403–518) remain the post-M0 roadmap.

---

## Verification

End-to-end checks the implementer should run before declaring M0 done:

1. **Local green:** `bundle install && bin/rails db:setup && bundle exec rspec` — all specs pass.
2. **Local smoke:** `bin/rails server`, visit `/`, `/people`, `/people/1`, `/events`, `/events/1`. Create a Person via the form, edit, delete. Create an Event linked to a Person. Flash messages appear. Navbar links consistent.
3. **Seed count:** `bin/rails runner 'puts Person.count + Event.count'` prints ≥ 20.
4. **Bootstrap assets load:** open the site in a browser, confirm navbar is styled, tables striped, forms `form-control`. Check browser devtools — no 404s on CSS/JS.
5. **CI green:** push to a branch, open PR, confirm GitHub Actions run passes.
6. **Heroku live:** `heroku open` loads the app; `heroku logs --tail` shows no errors; `/people` shows seeded data.
7. **Deliverable checklist walk-through:** go through `initial-deliverable.txt` line by line, confirm each bullet met. Record in PR description.
8. **All-team commits:** `git shortlog -sne` shows commits from all four GitHub handles.

# Milestone 0 Implementation Plan — project-stay-in-touch

## Context

**What is this?** A Rails app for the Northwestern CS Software Studio class, "stay-in-touch" / Serendipity: a tool that helps people intentionally maintain friendships by tracking who they want to stay in touch with and how often, plus a log of actual catch-ups. The long-term vision (Python prototype in `claude-initial-files/`) uses Google Calendar + Forms + a daily scheduler to auto-pair friends. **Milestone 0 is not that.** M0 is the minimum viable Rails foundation on which future milestones layer features.

**Why now?** The repo currently contains only `initial-deliverable.txt` and the Python concept files. None of the M0 requirements are met: no Rails app, no models, no tests, no CI, no Heroku, no README/wiki. This plan produces a complete M0 submission.

**Outcome:** A Rails 7 app with two resources (Person, Event), full CRUD, Bootstrap UI, 20+ seeds, RSpec tests running in GitHub Actions, deployed to Heroku, documented in `README.md` and `wiki.md`.

**Team (GitHub):** `AMindToThink` (Matthew), `akurdia`, `josyao1`, `mjgaughan`. Repo: `NU-CS-Software-Studio-Spring-26/project-stay-in-touch`.

**Design principles (user-stated):** well-documented, well-structured, maintainable, extensible. Concretely: thin controllers, fat-but-focused models with validations, shared layout/partials, named routes via `resources`, RSpec model + request specs, YARD-style doc comments only where behavior isn't obvious from names, all domain logic discoverable from `app/models/` and `app/services/`.

---

## Tech Stack

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Ruby | 3.3.x (latest Heroku-supported patch) | Supported on Heroku-24 stack; matches class expectation of current Ruby. |
| Rails | 7.2.x | Stable, Heroku-ready, importmap + propshaft defaults work with Bootstrap via `bootstrap` gem. |
| DB (dev/test) | SQLite | Zero setup for teammates cloning the repo. |
| DB (prod) | PostgreSQL | Required on Heroku; configured only in `production` group. |
| Frontend CSS | `bootstrap` gem + `sassc-rails` | First-party SCSS; no CDN dependency; works offline and on Heroku. |
| JS bundling | importmap-rails (Rails default) | No Node build on Heroku, keeps buildpack simple. |
| Tests | RSpec (`rspec-rails`, `factory_bot_rails`, `shoulda-matchers`) | Matches the class's `hw2` pattern (workflow runs `bundle exec rspec`). |
| CI | GitHub Actions (`ruby/setup-ruby`) | Mirrors hw2's `ruby.yml` workflow so graders see familiar output. |
| Lint (optional, nice-to-have) | `rubocop-rails-omakase` | Rails 7.2 default; keeps style consistent across four contributors. |

---

## Data Model

Two primary domain models (`Person`, `Event`) joined many-to-many via an `EventParticipant` join model. The deliverable requires a minimum of 2 models and says associations are "welcome"; the join model is pure plumbing and keeps the design extensible (future fields: `role`, `rsvp_status`, `was_organizer`).

### `Person`
A person you want to stay in touch with.

| Field | Type | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| `name` | string | `presence: true` |
| `email` | string | `presence: true`, `uniqueness: true` (case-insensitive), format validation |
| `timezone` | string | `presence: true`, inclusion in `ActiveSupport::TimeZone.all.map(&:name)`, default `"America/Chicago"` |
| `preferred_start_hour` | integer | `0..23`, must be `<= preferred_end_hour` |
| `preferred_end_hour` | integer | `0..23` |
| `frequency_weeks` | decimal(5,2) | `> 0`, default `4.0` — how often you'd like to catch up |
| `notes` | text | optional |

```ruby
has_many :event_participants, dependent: :destroy
has_many :events, through: :event_participants
```

Helper methods for later reuse (kept tiny, documented):
- `#latest_event` → `events.order(occurred_at: :desc).first`
- `#days_until_due` → `frequency_weeks * 7 - days_since(latest_event&.occurred_at)` (returns `nil` if no events; negative when overdue). Exposed as a read-only field on the index so the app already feels purposeful even under the "Just the data + UI" M0 scope — the number is computed on the fly, no extra schema.

### `Event` (renamed from "Interaction" per user)
A logged catch-up — a call, coffee, group dinner, etc. Can involve one or many People.

| Field | Type | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| `occurred_at` | datetime | `presence: true`, `<= Time.current` |
| `medium` | string | `presence: true`, inclusion in `%w[call coffee text video in_person other]` |
| `title` | string | optional short label (e.g., "Birthday dinner") |
| `notes` | text | optional |

```ruby
has_many :event_participants, dependent: :destroy
has_many :people, through: :event_participants
validates :people, presence: true  # must have ≥1 participant
```

Scopes:
- `Event.recent` → `order(occurred_at: :desc)`

### `EventParticipant` (join model)
Pure join table; one row per (Person, Event) pair.

| Field | Type | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| `person_id` | bigint (FK) | `presence: true`, index, composite unique with `event_id` |
| `event_id` | bigint (FK) | `presence: true`, index, composite unique with `person_id` |

```ruby
belongs_to :person
belongs_to :event
validates :person_id, uniqueness: { scope: :event_id }
```

No UI is dedicated to `EventParticipant` — participants are managed via checkboxes on the Event form. It's intentionally not one of the "4 views" called out in the deliverable; the 4 views remain People index/show + Events index/show.

### Migrations (generated via `rails g model`)
- `db/migrate/<ts>_create_people.rb`
- `db/migrate/<ts>_create_events.rb` (no `person_id` FK — participants live in the join table)
- `db/migrate/<ts>_create_event_participants.rb` (`t.references :person`, `t.references :event`, `add_index ..., [:person_id, :event_id], unique: true`)

---

## Routes & Views

`config/routes.rb`:
```ruby
root "people#index"
resources :people
resources :events
```

This produces the 4 **required** views explicitly called out in the deliverable (index + show per model) plus the new/edit views needed for CRUD:

| Route | View | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `GET /people` | `people/index` | Table of all people with name, email, frequency, days-until-due, actions |
| `GET /people/:id` | `people/show` | Single person + their events timeline (via `person.events`) |
| `GET /events` | `events/index` | Table of all events, showing participants as comma-separated links |
| `GET /events/:id` | `events/show` | Single event detail with all participants listed as linked names |
| `new`/`edit`/`create`/`update`/`destroy` | standard Rails | CRUD completeness |

**Consistency across views** (deliverable requirement: "feel of one product"):
- Shared `app/views/layouts/application.html.erb` with a Bootstrap navbar linking **People**, **Events**, and **Add Person / Add Event**.
- Shared `_flash.html.erb` partial for notices/alerts.
- Shared `_form.html.erb` partials per resource. Event form uses a Bootstrap `.form-check` list of checkboxes (one per Person) bound to `event.person_ids` via a `people_ids: []` strong param.
- `app/views/shared/_breadcrumbs.html.erb` for show/edit pages.
- Every show page has "Back to index," "Edit," and "Delete" buttons wired consistently.

---

## Bootstrap UI

- Install via `bootstrap` gem (`~> 5.3`) + `sassc-rails`.
- Replace `app/assets/stylesheets/application.css` with `application.scss` that `@import "bootstrap"` and a small `custom.scss` partial for project-specific tweaks.
- Import Bootstrap JS bundle via importmap (`bin/importmap pin bootstrap @popperjs/core`).
- Required Bootstrap elements actually used (matching deliverable "at least table, buttons, form, the more the better"):
  - **table**: index pages use `.table .table-striped .table-hover`
  - **buttons**: all action buttons use `.btn .btn-primary` / `.btn-outline-danger` / etc.
  - **form**: new/edit use `.form-control`, `.form-label`, `.form-select`, `.form-text`
  - **navbar**: responsive `.navbar .navbar-expand-lg` in the layout
  - **card**: show pages wrap person/event details in `.card`
  - **badge**: show event medium as a colored `.badge`

---

## Seed Data

`db/seeds.rb` creates **12 people + 15 events + ~25 event_participants ≈ 52 rows total**, well above the 20-entry minimum (the deliverable's "entries" likely means user-visible domain rows — People + Events alone already hit 27).

Structure:
- 12 `Person` rows with varied timezones (Chicago, NYC, LA, London, Tokyo), preferred hours, frequencies (1–8 weeks), realistic notes.
- 15 `Event` rows spread across the past 3 months, across all media (`call`, `coffee`, `text`, `video`, `in_person`, `other`).
- Mix of solo events (1 participant) and group events (2–4 participants) so the many-to-many is exercised: e.g., "Birthday dinner" links 4 people; most calls link 1 person.
- Created via `Event.create!(..., people: [alice, bob])` so ActiveRecord inserts the join rows automatically.
- Script is idempotent: `EventParticipant.destroy_all; Event.destroy_all; Person.destroy_all` at the top (in that order to respect FK constraints) so `rails db:seed` can be re-run safely.
- Uses plain Ruby arrays of hashes — no Faker dependency — so output is stable/reviewable.

---

## Testing (RSpec)

Goals: cover validations, associations, and the key controller paths. Generated with AI assistance as the deliverable explicitly allows.

Structure (mirrors hw2 conventions):
```
spec/
├── rails_helper.rb
├── spec_helper.rb
├── models/
│   ├── person_spec.rb             # validations, associations, #days_until_due, #latest_event
│   ├── event_spec.rb              # validations, associations, scopes, "must have ≥1 person"
│   └── event_participant_spec.rb  # join-uniqueness, presence of both FKs
├── requests/
│   ├── people_spec.rb      # GET index/show/new/edit, POST create, PATCH update, DELETE destroy
│   └── events_spec.rb      # same, including create/update with people_ids: [...] param
└── factories/
    ├── people.rb
    ├── events.rb                  # factory builds events with 1 participant by default via transient
    └── event_participants.rb
```

- `shoulda-matchers` for compact validation/association specs.
- `factory_bot_rails` for test data.
- `DatabaseCleaner` not needed — use Rails transactional fixtures.
- Target: ~30 specs total, green locally via `bundle exec rspec`.

---

## Continuous Integration

File: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (modeled on hw2's `ruby.yml`).

```yaml
name: CI
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: ruby/setup-ruby@v1
        with:
          ruby-version: '3.3'
          bundler-cache: true
      - run: bin/rails db:prepare
      - run: bundle exec rspec
```

Badge in README links back to Actions runs. Branch protection is out of scope for M0 but can be added when team agrees.

---

## Heroku Deployment

- `Procfile`: `web: bundle exec rails server -p $PORT -e $RAILS_ENV` + `release: bundle exec rails db:migrate`.
- `config/database.yml` uses `ENV["DATABASE_URL"]` in production (Rails default already does this).
- Add `pg` gem to `:production` group, keep `sqlite3` in `:development, :test`.
- `bundle lock --add-platform x86_64-linux` so Heroku's Linux platform resolves gems.
- Provision: `heroku create stay-in-touch-<suffix>` (team picks suffix), add `heroku-postgresql:mini` add-on, push, `heroku run rails db:seed`.
- Stack: `heroku-24` (default for new apps in 2026).
- Document exact commands in README so teammates can redeploy.

---

## Documentation

### `README.md` (required)
Sections:
1. **MVP statement** — one paragraph from Serendipity product doc, trimmed: "A web app that helps you intentionally stay in touch by tracking the people who matter to you, how often you'd like to reach out, and your history with them."
2. **Team** — table of names + GitHub handles (`@AMindToThink`, `@akurdia`, `@josyao1`, `@mjgaughan`).
3. **Live app** — Heroku URL.
4. **Local development** — `bundle install`, `bin/rails db:setup`, `bin/rails server`.
5. **Tests** — `bundle exec rspec`.
6. **Deployment** — Heroku steps.
7. **Communication section (required verbatim by deliverable):**
   - Primary channel (TBD during kickoff — group chat platform)
   - Standup cadence
   - Decision rule (e.g., lazy consensus within 24 h, else majority vote)
   - How we split work (GitHub Issues assigned per feature)
   - Response-time expectation
8. **CI badge**.

### `wiki.md` (required)
Sections:
1. **Problem** — distilled from `claude-initial-files/serendipity-product-report.md`.
2. **Miro board link** — OO design (placeholder link for team to fill in).
3. **Object-oriented design overview** — class diagram described in text: Person ↔ Event association, future `User`, `Pair`, `ScheduledCall` classes sketched.
4. **Future features (post-M0)** — user auth, mutual-pair matching, Google Calendar integration, automatic scheduler (cron), notification emails, admin dashboard.
5. **Similar products** — Monaru, Dex, Clay, UpHabit, Nat.app — with one-line differentiators.
6. **References** — link back to the original Python prototype in `claude-initial-files/` for historical context.

### `.gitignore`
Use Rails's generated `.gitignore` + explicit entries for `.DS_Store`, `*.swp`, `.env*`, `/tmp/*`, `/log/*`, `/storage/*`, `/public/assets`, and `/coverage`.

---

## File / Directory Layout After Implementation

```
project-stay-in-touch/
├── .github/workflows/ci.yml         # NEW
├── .gitignore                       # NEW (Rails default + additions)
├── Gemfile / Gemfile.lock           # NEW
├── Procfile                         # NEW (Heroku)
├── README.md                        # NEW
├── wiki.md                          # NEW
├── Rakefile, config.ru, bin/, etc.  # Rails generated
├── app/
│   ├── controllers/
│   │   ├── application_controller.rb
│   │   ├── people_controller.rb
│   │   └── events_controller.rb
│   ├── models/
│   │   ├── person.rb                # with #days_until_due, validations
│   │   └── event.rb                 # with scopes, validations
│   ├── views/
│   │   ├── layouts/application.html.erb   # Bootstrap navbar + flash
│   │   ├── shared/_breadcrumbs.html.erb
│   │   ├── people/{index,show,new,edit,_form,_person}.html.erb
│   │   └── events/{index,show,new,edit,_form,_event}.html.erb
│   └── assets/stylesheets/application.scss  # @import "bootstrap"
├── config/
│   ├── routes.rb                    # root + two resources
│   ├── database.yml
│   └── importmap.rb                 # pins bootstrap, @popperjs/core
├── db/
│   ├── migrate/*_create_people.rb
│   ├── migrate/*_create_events.rb
│   ├── schema.rb
│   └── seeds.rb                     # 12 people + 15+ events
├── spec/
│   ├── rails_helper.rb
│   ├── spec_helper.rb
│   ├── models/{person,event}_spec.rb
│   ├── requests/{people,events}_spec.rb
│   └── factories/{people,events}.rb
└── claude-initial-files/            # PRESERVED (Python prototype + product doc)
    └── (unchanged — referenced by wiki.md)
```

`initial-deliverable.txt` can stay (useful reference) or be deleted — team's call.

---

## Implementation Order (recommended for teammate hand-off)

Keep each step individually committable so commits from all team members are easy to distribute.

1. `rails new . --database=sqlite3 --css=bootstrap --skip-bundle` in-place, then `bundle install`. Commit.
2. Add `.gitignore` additions, RSpec + FactoryBot + Shoulda, `rails g rspec:install`. Commit.
3. `rails g model Person ...`, `rails g model Event ...`, `rails g model EventParticipant person:references event:references`, add composite unique index in the migration, `db:migrate`, validations, model specs. Commit.
4. `rails g scaffold_controller` (or hand-written controllers) for People + Events; Event controller permits `people_ids: []`; request specs covering create/update with multiple participants. Commit.
5. Views: layout, navbar, Bootstrap index/show/new/edit, `_form` partials. Commit.
6. `db/seeds.rb` with 12 people + 15+ events; `rails db:seed`; manual smoke in browser. Commit.
7. `.github/workflows/ci.yml`; push and confirm green. Commit.
8. Heroku app create, `Procfile`, `pg` gem, `bundle lock --add-platform`, deploy, `heroku run rails db:seed`. Commit.
9. `README.md` + `wiki.md` with Heroku URL, CI badge, Communication section, MVP, team. Commit.
10. Distribute small follow-up commits across teammates to satisfy "commits from all team members" (e.g., assign each teammate a refinement — new seed data, CSS tweak, test addition, wiki section).

---

## Critical Files to Modify / Create

Most of these are **generated** by Rails — the plan is mainly about what the generators produce and what we fill in.

- `Gemfile` — add `bootstrap`, `sassc-rails`, `rspec-rails`, `factory_bot_rails`, `shoulda-matchers`, `pg` (:production), `sqlite3` (:dev/:test).
- `app/models/person.rb` — validations + `has_many :events, through: :event_participants` + `#days_until_due`, `#latest_event`.
- `app/models/event.rb` — validations (including ≥1 participant) + `has_many :people, through: :event_participants` + scopes.
- `app/models/event_participant.rb` — join with uniqueness validation.
- `app/controllers/people_controller.rb`, `events_controller.rb` — standard 7 REST actions with strong params; `events_controller` permits `people_ids: []`.
- `app/views/layouts/application.html.erb` — Bootstrap navbar, flash partial.
- `db/seeds.rb` — 12 + 15.
- `spec/models/*`, `spec/requests/*`, `spec/factories/*`.
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `.gitignore`, `Procfile`, `README.md`, `wiki.md`.

---

## Domain Logic Re-Used from the Python Prototype

Preserved as future hooks (not implemented in M0, but data model supports them):
- `frequency_weeks` on `Person` — directly maps to the "how often" concept in `scheduler.py`.
- `occurred_at` on `Event` — feeds future `last_calls` equivalent.
- `preferred_start_hour` / `preferred_end_hour` — future availability windowing.
- `timezone` — future Calendar integration.

In wiki.md's "Future features," we document that the geometric-mean pairing, random jitter, and Google Calendar lookup (from `claude-initial-files/scheduler.py` lines 403–518) remain the post-M0 roadmap.

---

## Verification

End-to-end checks the implementer should run before declaring M0 done:

1. **Local green:** `bundle install && bin/rails db:setup && bundle exec rspec` — all specs pass.
2. **Local smoke:** `bin/rails server`, visit `/`, `/people`, `/people/1`, `/events`, `/events/1`. Create a Person via the form, edit, delete. Create an Event linked to a Person. Flash messages appear. Navbar links consistent.
3. **Seed count:** `bin/rails runner 'puts Person.count + Event.count'` prints ≥ 20.
4. **Bootstrap assets load:** open the site in a browser, confirm navbar is styled, tables striped, forms `form-control`. Check browser devtools — no 404s on CSS/JS.
5. **CI green:** push to a branch, open PR, confirm GitHub Actions run passes.
6. **Heroku live:** `heroku open` loads the app; `heroku logs --tail` shows no errors; `/people` shows seeded data.
7. **Deliverable checklist walk-through:** go through `initial-deliverable.txt` line by line, confirm each bullet met. Record in PR description.
8. **All-team commits:** `git shortlog -sne` shows commits from all four GitHub handles.

# Milestone 0 Implementation Plan — project-stay-in-touch

## Context

**What is this?** A Rails app for the Northwestern CS Software Studio class, "stay-in-touch" / Serendipity: a tool that helps people intentionally maintain friendships by tracking who they want to stay in touch with and how often, plus a log of actual catch-ups. The long-term vision (Python prototype in `claude-initial-files/`) uses Google Calendar + Forms + a daily scheduler to auto-pair friends. **Milestone 0 is not that.** M0 is the minimum viable Rails foundation on which future milestones layer features.

**Why now?** The repo currently contains only `initial-deliverable.txt` and the Python concept files. None of the M0 requirements are met: no Rails app, no models, no tests, no CI, no Heroku, no README/wiki. This plan produces a complete M0 submission.

**Outcome:** A Rails 7 app with two resources (Person, Event), full CRUD, Bootstrap UI, 20+ seeds, RSpec tests running in GitHub Actions, deployed to Heroku, documented in `README.md` and `wiki.md`.

---

## Bootstrap UI

- Install via `bootstrap` gem (`~> 5.3`) + `sassc-rails`.
- Replace `app/assets/stylesheets/application.css` with `application.scss` that `@import "bootstrap"` and a small `custom.scss` partial for project-specific tweaks.
- Import Bootstrap JS bundle via importmap (`bin/importmap pin bootstrap @popperjs/core`).
- Required Bootstrap elements actually used (matching deliverable "at least table, buttons, form, the more the better"):
  - **table**: index pages use `.table .table-striped .table-hover`
  - **buttons**: all action buttons use `.btn .btn-primary` / `.btn-outline-danger` / etc.
  - **form**: new/edit use `.form-control`, `.form-label`, `.form-select`, `.form-text`
  - **navbar**: responsive `.navbar .navbar-expand-lg` in the layout
  - **card**: show pages wrap person/event details in `.card`
  - **badge**: show event medium as a colored `.badge`

---

## Seed Data

`db/seeds.rb` creates **12 people + 15 events + ~25 event_participants ≈ 52 rows total**, well above the 20-entry minimum (the deliverable's "entries" likely means user-visible domain rows — People + Events alone already hit 27).

Structure:
- 12 `Person` rows with varied timezones (Chicago, NYC, LA, London, Tokyo), preferred hours, frequencies (1–8 weeks), realistic notes.
- 15 `Event` rows spread across the past 3 months, across all media (`call`, `coffee`, `text`, `video`, `in_person`, `other`).
- Mix of solo events (1 participant) and group events (2–4 participants) so the many-to-many is exercised: e.g., "Birthday dinner" links 4 people; most calls link 1 person.
- Created via `Event.create!(..., people: [alice, bob])` so ActiveRecord inserts the join rows automatically.
- Script is idempotent: `EventParticipant.destroy_all; Event.destroy_all; Person.destroy_all` at the top (in that order to respect FK constraints) so `rails db:seed` can be re-run safely.
- Uses plain Ruby arrays of hashes — no Faker dependency — so output is stable/reviewable.

---

## Testing (RSpec)

Goals: cover validations, associations, and the key controller paths. Generated with AI assistance as the deliverable explicitly allows.

Structure (mirrors hw2 conventions):
```
spec/
├── rails_helper.rb
├── spec_helper.rb
├── models/
│   ├── person_spec.rb             # validations, associations, #days_until_due, #latest_event
│   ├── event_spec.rb              # validations, associations, scopes, "must have ≥1 person"
│   └── event_participant_spec.rb  # join-uniqueness, presence of both FKs
├── requests/
│   ├── people_spec.rb      # GET index/show/new/edit, POST create, PATCH update, DELETE destroy
│   └── events_spec.rb      # same, including create/update with people_ids: [...] param
└── factories/
    ├── people.rb
    ├── events.rb                  # factory builds events with 1 participant by default via transient
    └── event_participants.rb
```

- `shoulda-matchers` for compact validation/association specs.
- `factory_bot_rails` for test data.
- `DatabaseCleaner` not needed — use Rails transactional fixtures.
- Target: ~30 specs total, green locally via `bundle exec rspec`.

---

## Continuous Integration

File: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (modeled on hw2's `ruby.yml`).

```yaml
name: CI
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: ruby/setup-ruby@v1
        with:
          ruby-version: '3.3'
          bundler-cache: true
      - run: bin/rails db:prepare
      - run: bundle exec rspec
```

Badge in README links back to Actions runs. Branch protection is out of scope for M0 but can be added when team agrees.

---

## Heroku Deployment

- `Procfile`: `web: bundle exec rails server -p $PORT -e $RAILS_ENV` + `release: bundle exec rails db:migrate`.
- `config/database.yml` uses `ENV["DATABASE_URL"]` in production (Rails default already does this).
- Add `pg` gem to `:production` group, keep `sqlite3` in `:development, :test`.
- `bundle lock --add-platform x86_64-linux` so Heroku's Linux platform resolves gems.
- Provision: `heroku create stay-in-touch-<suffix>` (team picks suffix), add `heroku-postgresql:mini` add-on, push, `heroku run rails db:seed`.
- Stack: `heroku-24` (default for new apps in 2026).
- Document exact commands in README so teammates can redeploy.

---

## Documentation

### `README.md` (required)
Sections:
1. **MVP statement** — one paragraph from Serendipity product doc, trimmed: "A web app that helps you intentionally stay in touch by tracking the people who matter to you, how often you'd like to reach out, and your history with them."
2. **Team** — table of names + GitHub handles (`@AMindToThink`, `@akurdia`, `@josyao1`, `@mjgaughan`).
3. **Live app** — Heroku URL.
4. **Local development** — `bundle install`, `bin/rails db:setup`, `bin/rails server`.
5. **Tests** — `bundle exec rspec`.
6. **Deployment** — Heroku steps.
7. **Communication section (required verbatim by deliverable):**
   - Primary channel (TBD during kickoff — group chat platform)
   - Standup cadence
   - Decision rule (e.g., lazy consensus within 24 h, else majority vote)
   - How we split work (GitHub Issues assigned per feature)
   - Response-time expectation
8. **CI badge**.

### `wiki.md` (required)
Sections:
1. **Problem** — distilled from `claude-initial-files/serendipity-product-report.md`.
2. **Miro board link** — OO design (placeholder link for team to fill in).
3. **Object-oriented design overview** — class diagram described in text: Person ↔ Event association, future `User`, `Pair`, `ScheduledCall` classes sketched.
4. **Future features (post-M0)** — user auth, mutual-pair matching, Google Calendar integration, automatic scheduler (cron), notification emails, admin dashboard.
5. **Similar products** — Monaru, Dex, Clay, UpHabit, Nat.app — with one-line differentiators.
6. **References** — link back to the original Python prototype in `claude-initial-files/` for historical context.

### `.gitignore`
Use Rails's generated `.gitignore` + explicit entries for `.DS_Store`, `*.swp`, `.env*`, `/tmp/*`, `/log/*`, `/storage/*`, `/public/assets`, and `/coverage`.

## Implementation Order (recommended for teammate hand-off)

Keep each step individually committable so commits from all team members are easy to distribute.

1. `rails new . --database=sqlite3 --css=bootstrap --skip-bundle` in-place, then `bundle install`. Commit.
2. Add `.gitignore` additions, RSpec + FactoryBot + Shoulda, `rails g rspec:install`. Commit.
3. `rails g model Person ...`, `rails g model Event ...`, `rails g model EventParticipant person:references event:references`, add composite unique index in the migration, `db:migrate`, validations, model specs. Commit.
4. `rails g scaffold_controller` (or hand-written controllers) for People + Events; Event controller permits `people_ids: []`; request specs covering create/update with multiple participants. Commit.
5. Views: layout, navbar, Bootstrap index/show/new/edit, `_form` partials. Commit.
6. `db/seeds.rb` with 12 people + 15+ events; `rails db:seed`; manual smoke in browser. Commit.
7. `.github/workflows/ci.yml`; push and confirm green. Commit.
8. Heroku app create, `Procfile`, `pg` gem, `bundle lock --add-platform`, deploy, `heroku run rails db:seed`. Commit.
9. `README.md` + `wiki.md` with Heroku URL, CI badge, Communication section, MVP, team. Commit.
10. Distribute small follow-up commits across teammates to satisfy "commits from all team members" (e.g., assign each teammate a refinement — new seed data, CSS tweak, test addition, wiki section).

---

## Critical Files to Modify / Create

Most of these are **generated** by Rails — the plan is mainly about what the generators produce and what we fill in.

- `Gemfile` — add `bootstrap`, `sassc-rails`, `rspec-rails`, `factory_bot_rails`, `shoulda-matchers`, `pg` (:production), `sqlite3` (:dev/:test).
- `app/models/person.rb` — validations + `has_many :events, through: :event_participants` + `#days_until_due`, `#latest_event`.
- `app/models/event.rb` — validations (including ≥1 participant) + `has_many :people, through: :event_participants` + scopes.
- `app/models/event_participant.rb` — join with uniqueness validation.
- `app/controllers/people_controller.rb`, `events_controller.rb` — standard 7 REST actions with strong params; `events_controller` permits `people_ids: []`.
- `app/views/layouts/application.html.erb` — Bootstrap navbar, flash partial.
- `db/seeds.rb` — 12 + 15.
- `spec/models/*`, `spec/requests/*`, `spec/factories/*`.
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `.gitignore`, `Procfile`, `README.md`, `wiki.md`.

---

## Domain Logic Re-Used from the Python Prototype

Preserved as future hooks (not implemented in M0, but data model supports them):
- `frequency_weeks` on `Person` — directly maps to the "how often" concept in `scheduler.py`.
- `occurred_at` on `Event` — feeds future `last_calls` equivalent.
- `preferred_start_hour` / `preferred_end_hour` — future availability windowing.
- `timezone` — future Calendar integration.

In wiki.md's "Future features," we document that the geometric-mean pairing, random jitter, and Google Calendar lookup (from `claude-initial-files/scheduler.py` lines 403–518) remain the post-M0 roadmap.

---

## Verification

End-to-end checks the implementer should run before declaring M0 done:

1. **Local green:** `bundle install && bin/rails db:setup && bundle exec rspec` — all specs pass.
2. **Local smoke:** `bin/rails server`, visit `/`, `/people`, `/people/1`, `/events`, `/events/1`. Create a Person via the form, edit, delete. Create an Event linked to a Person. Flash messages appear. Navbar links consistent.
3. **Seed count:** `bin/rails runner 'puts Person.count + Event.count'` prints ≥ 20.
4. **Bootstrap assets load:** open the site in a browser, confirm navbar is styled, tables striped, forms `form-control`. Check browser devtools — no 404s on CSS/JS.
5. **CI green:** push to a branch, open PR, confirm GitHub Actions run passes.
6. **Heroku live:** `heroku open` loads the app; `heroku logs --tail` shows no errors; `/people` shows seeded data.
7. **Deliverable checklist walk-through:** go through `initial-deliverable.txt` line by line, confirm each bullet met. Record in PR description.
8. **All-team commits:** `git shortlog -sne` shows commits from all four GitHub handles.

# This file is copied to spec/ when you run 'rails generate rspec:install'
require 'spec_helper'
ENV['RAILS_ENV'] ||= 'test'
require_relative '../config/environment'
# Prevent database truncation if the environment is production
abort("The Rails environment is running in production mode!") if Rails.env.production?
# Uncomment the line below in case you have `--require rails_helper` in the `.rspec` file
# that will avoid rails generators crashing because migrations haven't been run yet
# return unless Rails.env.test?
require 'rspec/rails'
# Add additional requires below this line. Rails is not loaded until this point!

# Requires supporting ruby files with custom matchers and macros, etc, in
# spec/support/ and its subdirectories. Files matching `spec/**/*_spec.rb` are
# run as spec files by default. This means that files in spec/support that end
# in _spec.rb will both be required and run as specs, causing the specs to be
# run twice. It is recommended that you do not name files matching this glob to
# end with _spec.rb. You can configure this pattern with the --pattern
# option on the command line or in ~/.rspec, .rspec or `.rspec-local`.
#
# The following line is provided for convenience purposes. It has the downside
# of increasing the boot-up time by auto-requiring all files in the support
# directory. Alternatively, in the individual `*_spec.rb` files, manually
# require only the support files necessary.
#
# Rails.root.glob('spec/support/**/*.rb').sort_by(&:to_s).each { |f| require f }

# Checks for pending migrations and applies them before tests are run.
# If you are not using ActiveRecord, you can remove these lines.
begin
  ActiveRecord::Migration.maintain_test_schema!
rescue ActiveRecord::PendingMigrationError => e
  abort e.to_s.strip
end
RSpec.configure do |config|
  # Remove this line if you're not using ActiveRecord or ActiveRecord fixtures
  config.fixture_paths = [
    Rails.root.join('spec/fixtures')
  ]

  # If you're not using ActiveRecord, or you'd prefer not to run each of your
  # examples within a transaction, remove the following line or assign false
  # instead of true.
  config.use_transactional_fixtures = true

  # You can uncomment this line to turn off ActiveRecord support entirely.
  # config.use_active_record = false

  # RSpec Rails uses metadata to mix in different behaviours to your tests,
  # for example enabling you to call `get` and `post` in request specs. e.g.:
  #
  #     RSpec.describe UsersController, type: :request do
  #       # ...
  #     end
  #
  # The different available types are documented in the features, such as in
  # https://rspec.info/features/7-1/rspec-rails
  #
  # You can also this infer these behaviours automatically by location, e.g.
  # /spec/models would pull in the same behaviour as `type: :model` but this
  # behaviour is considered legacy and will be removed in a future version.
  #
  # To enable this behaviour uncomment the line below.
  # config.infer_spec_type_from_file_location!

  # Filter lines from Rails gems in backtraces.
  config.filter_rails_from_backtrace!
  # arbitrary gems may also be filtered via:
  # config.filter_gems_from_backtrace("gem name")
end

# Shoulda-matchers: use with RSpec + Rails for concise validation/association specs.

    with.library :rails

end

content.subject: Build Bootstrap views (layout, navbar, index/show/new/edit/_form)
content.description: Shared layout with navbar + flash, people and events index/show/new/edit, _form partials, shared breadcrumbs.

content.subject: Write db/seeds.rb with 12 people + 15 events
content.description: Idempotent seed script; verify counts >= 20.

content.subject: Write README.md and wiki.md
content.description: README with MVP, team, live-app placeholder, setup, tests, deployment, Communication section, CI badge. wiki.md with problem, OO design, future features, similar products.

content.subject: Create feature branch and verify Ruby toolchain
content.description: Create a feature branch off main, verify Ruby/Rails/bundler versions available on this machine.
content.activeForm: Setting up branch and verifying toolchain

content.subject: Local verification: rspec + smoke browse
content.description: Run full spec suite, launch server, smoke-test all CRUD flows end-to-end, verify Bootstrap assets load, report results.

content.subject: Scaffold Rails app and install gems
content.description: rails new . --database=sqlite3 --css=bootstrap --skip-bundle; add gems (rspec-rails, factory_bot_rails, shoulda-matchers, pg prod); bundle install.

content.subject: Configure RSpec and testing harness
content.description: rails g rspec:install; add shoulda-matchers + factory_bot config to rails_helper.rb; update .gitignore.

content.subject: Add GitHub Actions CI workflow
content.description: .github/workflows/ci.yml modeled on hw2; run specs on push and PR.

content.subject: Generate Person, Event, EventParticipant models + migrations
content.description: rails g model Person + Event + EventParticipant, add composite unique index, db:migrate, validations, associations, helper methods, model specs.

content.subject: Build PeopleController and EventsController + request specs
content.description: Controllers with 7 REST actions, strong params (events permits people_ids:[]); request specs.

content.subject: Add Heroku deployment config (Procfile, pg gem, platform lock)
content.description: Procfile with web + release, pg gem in :production, bundle lock --add-platform x86_64-linux. Do not deploy without user approval.

input.content:
# A Person you want to stay in touch with. Stores contact identity plus the
# two inputs that drive future reach-out reminders (Serendipity roadmap):
# the preferred-hours window (for availability matching) and a target
# `frequency_weeks` (how often you'd like to catch up).
class Person < ApplicationRecord
  VALID_MEDIA_FREQUENCY_RANGE = (0.01..520.0).freeze
  HOUR_RANGE = (0..23).freeze
  EMAIL_FORMAT = URI::MailTo::EMAIL_REGEXP

  has_many :event_participants, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :events, through: :event_participants

  validates :name, presence: true
  validates :email,
            presence: true,
            format: { with: EMAIL_FORMAT },
            uniqueness: { case_sensitive: false }
  validates :timezone,
            presence: true,
            inclusion: { in: ActiveSupport::TimeZone.all.map(&:name) }
  validates :preferred_start_hour,
            presence: true,
            numericality: { only_integer: true, in: HOUR_RANGE }
  validates :preferred_end_hour,
            presence: true,
            numericality: { only_integer: true, in: HOUR_RANGE }
  validates :frequency_weeks,
            presence: true,
            numericality: { greater_than: 0, less_than_or_equal_to: 520 }
  validate  :preferred_window_ordered

  # Most recent Event for this Person, or nil if none yet.
  def latest_event
    events.order(occurred_at: :desc).first
  end

  # Days until the next reach-out is "due" per frequency_weeks.
  # Negative values mean overdue; nil when no prior events.
  def days_until_due
    return nil unless latest_event

    deadline = latest_event.occurred_at + (frequency_weeks * 7).days
    ((deadline - Time.current) / 1.day).round
  end

  private

  def preferred_window_ordered
    return if preferred_start_hour.nil? || preferred_end_hour.nil?
    return if preferred_start_hour <= preferred_end_hour

    errors.add(:preferred_end_hour, "must be greater than or equal to preferred_start_hour")
  end
end

class Event < ApplicationRecord
end

input.content:
# A logged catch-up — call, coffee, text, etc. One Event may involve many
# People (group dinner, conference call), so the join lives in
# EventParticipant rather than a direct belongs_to.
class Event < ApplicationRecord
  MEDIA = %w[call coffee text video in_person other].freeze

  has_many :event_participants, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :people, through: :event_participants

  validates :occurred_at, presence: true
  validate  :occurred_at_not_in_future
  validates :medium, presence: true, inclusion: { in: MEDIA }
  validate  :must_have_at_least_one_person

  scope :recent, -> { order(occurred_at: :desc) }

  # Display-friendly title fallback when a Person may have left the title blank.
  def display_title
    title.presence || "#{medium.titleize} on #{occurred_at.to_date}"
  end

  private

  def occurred_at_not_in_future
    return if occurred_at.blank?
    return if occurred_at <= Time.current

    errors.add(:occurred_at, "cannot be in the future")
  end

  def must_have_at_least_one_person
    return if event_participants.any? || people.any?

    errors.add(:base, "must include at least one person")
  end
end

# Join model connecting People to Events. Kept as an explicit AR model (rather
# than has_and_belongs_to_many) so future roadmap fields like rsvp_status,
# role, or was_organizer can be added without another migration-with-move.
class EventParticipant < ApplicationRecord
  belongs_to :person
  belongs_to :event

  validates :person_id, uniqueness: { scope: :event_id }
end

require "rails_helper"

RSpec.describe Person, type: :model do
  describe "associations" do
    it { is_expected.to have_many(:event_participants).dependent(:destroy) }
    it { is_expected.to have_many(:events).through(:event_participants) }
  end

  describe "validations" do
    subject { build(:person) }

    it { is_expected.to validate_presence_of(:name) }
    it { is_expected.to validate_presence_of(:email) }
    it { is_expected.to validate_uniqueness_of(:email).case_insensitive }
    it { is_expected.to validate_presence_of(:timezone) }
    it { is_expected.to allow_value("America/Chicago").for(:timezone) }
    it { is_expected.not_to allow_value("Not/A_Zone").for(:timezone) }
    it { is_expected.to validate_numericality_of(:preferred_start_hour).only_integer.is_in(0..23) }
    it { is_expected.to validate_numericality_of(:preferred_end_hour).only_integer.is_in(0..23) }
    it { is_expected.to validate_numericality_of(:frequency_weeks).is_greater_than(0) }

    it "rejects malformed email" do
      person = build(:person, email: "not-an-email")
      expect(person).not_to be_valid
    end

    it "requires preferred_end_hour >= preferred_start_hour" do
      person = build(:person, preferred_start_hour: 20, preferred_end_hour: 8)
      expect(person).not_to be_valid
      expect(person.errors[:preferred_end_hour]).to be_present
    end
  end

  describe "#latest_event" do
    let(:person) { create(:person) }

    it "returns nil when no events" do
      expect(person.latest_event).to be_nil
    end

    it "returns the most recent event" do
      older  = create(:event, occurred_at: 2.weeks.ago, people: [person])
      newer  = create(:event, occurred_at: 3.days.ago,  people: [person])
      _other = create(:event, occurred_at: 1.day.ago, people: [create(:person)])
      expect(person.latest_event).to eq(newer)
      expect(person.latest_event).not_to eq(older)
    end
  end

  describe "#days_until_due" do
    let(:person) { create(:person, frequency_weeks: 2.0) }

    it "returns nil when no events" do
      expect(person.days_until_due).to be_nil
    end

    it "is negative when overdue" do
      create(:event, occurred_at: 30.days.ago, people: [person])
      expect(person.days_until_due).to be < 0
    end

    it "is positive when upcoming" do
      create(:event, occurred_at: 1.day.ago, people: [person])
      expect(person.days_until_due).to be > 0
    end
  end
end

input.content:
require "rails_helper"

RSpec.describe Event, type: :model do
  describe "associations" do
    it { is_expected.to have_many(:event_participants).dependent(:destroy) }
    it { is_expected.to have_many(:people).through(:event_participants) }
  end

  describe "validations" do
    subject { build(:event) }

    it { is_expected.to validate_presence_of(:occurred_at) }
    it { is_expected.to validate_presence_of(:medium) }
    it { is_expected.to validate_inclusion_of(:medium).in_array(Event::MEDIA) }

    it "rejects occurred_at in the future" do
      event = build(:event, occurred_at: 1.day.from_now)
      expect(event).not_to be_valid
      expect(event.errors[:occurred_at]).to be_present
    end

    it "requires at least one person" do
      event = Event.new(occurred_at: 1.day.ago, medium: "call")
      expect(event).not_to be_valid
      expect(event.errors[:base]).to include("must include at least one person")
    end

    it "is valid with multiple people" do
      people = create_list(:person, 3)
      event  = build(:event, people: people)
      expect(event).to be_valid
    end
  end

  describe ".recent" do
    it "orders by occurred_at desc" do
      old_event = create(:event, occurred_at: 30.days.ago)
      new_event = create(:event, occurred_at: 1.day.ago)
      expect(Event.recent.to_a).to eq([new_event, old_event])
    end
  end

  describe "#display_title" do
    it "returns title when present" do
      event = build(:event, title: "Birthday dinner", occurred_at: Time.zone.local(2026, 1, 5))
      expect(event.display_title).to eq("Birthday dinner")
    end

    it "falls back to medium + date when title blank" do
      event = build(:event, title: "", medium: "call", occurred_at: Time.zone.local(2026, 1, 5))
      expect(event.display_title).to eq("Call on 2026-01-05")
    end
  end
end

require "rails_helper"

RSpec.describe EventParticipant, type: :model do
  describe "associations" do
    it { is_expected.to belong_to(:person) }
    it { is_expected.to belong_to(:event) }
  end

  describe "validations" do
    it "forbids the same person on the same event twice" do
      person = create(:person)
      event  = create(:event, people: [person])
      dup    = EventParticipant.new(person: person, event: event)
      expect(dup).not_to be_valid
      expect(dup.errors[:person_id]).to be_present
    end

    it "allows the same person across different events" do
      person  = create(:person)
      event_a = create(:event, people: [person])
      event_b = create(:event)
      ep      = EventParticipant.new(person: person, event: event_b)
      expect(ep).to be_valid
      expect(event_a).to be_valid
    end
  end
end

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# A Person you want to stay in touch with. Stores contact identity plus the
# two inputs that drive future reach-out reminders (Serendipity roadmap):
# the preferred-hours window (for availability matching) and a target
# `frequency_weeks` (how often you'd like to catch up).
class Person < ApplicationRecord
  HOUR_RANGE = (0..23).freeze
  EMAIL_FORMAT = URI::MailTo::EMAIL_REGEXP

  has_many :event_participants, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :events, through: :event_participants

  validates :name, presence: true
  validates :email,
            presence: true,
            format: { with: EMAIL_FORMAT },
            uniqueness: { case_sensitive: false }
  validates :timezone,
            presence: true,
            inclusion: { in: ActiveSupport::TimeZone.all.map(&:name) }
  validates :preferred_start_hour,
            presence: true,
            numericality: { only_integer: true, in: HOUR_RANGE }
  validates :preferred_end_hour,
            presence: true,
            numericality: { only_integer: true, in: HOUR_RANGE }
  validates :frequency_weeks,
            presence: true,
            numericality: { greater_than: 0, less_than_or_equal_to: 520 }
  validate  :preferred_window_ordered

  # Most recent Event for this Person, or nil if none yet.
  def latest_event
    events.order(occurred_at: :desc).first
  end

  # Days until the next reach-out is "due" per frequency_weeks.
  # Negative values mean overdue; nil when no prior events.
  def days_until_due
    return nil unless latest_event

    deadline = latest_event.occurred_at + (frequency_weeks * 7).days
    ((deadline - Time.current) / 1.day).round
  end

  private

  def preferred_window_ordered
    return if preferred_start_hour.nil? || preferred_end_hour.nil?
    return if preferred_start_hour <= preferred_end_hour

    errors.add(:preferred_end_hour, "must be greater than or equal to preferred_start_hour")
  end
end

require "rails_helper"

RSpec.describe Person, type: :model do
  describe "associations" do
    it { is_expected.to have_many(:event_participants).dependent(:destroy) }
    it { is_expected.to have_many(:events).through(:event_participants) }
  end

  describe "validations" do
    subject { build(:person) }

    it { is_expected.to validate_presence_of(:name) }
    it { is_expected.to validate_presence_of(:email) }
    it { is_expected.to validate_uniqueness_of(:email).case_insensitive }
    it { is_expected.to validate_presence_of(:timezone) }
    it { is_expected.to allow_value("America/Chicago").for(:timezone) }
    it { is_expected.not_to allow_value("Not/A_Zone").for(:timezone) }
    it { is_expected.to validate_numericality_of(:preferred_start_hour).only_integer.is_in(0..23) }
    it { is_expected.to validate_numericality_of(:preferred_end_hour).only_integer.is_in(0..23) }
    it { is_expected.to validate_numericality_of(:frequency_weeks).is_greater_than(0) }

    it "rejects malformed email" do
      person = build(:person, email: "not-an-email")
      expect(person).not_to be_valid
    end

    it "requires preferred_end_hour >= preferred_start_hour" do
      person = build(:person, preferred_start_hour: 20, preferred_end_hour: 8)
      expect(person).not_to be_valid
      expect(person.errors[:preferred_end_hour]).to be_present
    end
  end

  describe "#latest_event" do
    let(:person) { create(:person) }

    it "returns nil when no events" do
      expect(person.latest_event).to be_nil
    end

    it "returns the most recent event" do
      older  = create(:event, occurred_at: 2.weeks.ago, people: [person])
      newer  = create(:event, occurred_at: 3.days.ago,  people: [person])
      _other = create(:event, occurred_at: 1.day.ago, people: [create(:person)])
      expect(person.latest_event).to eq(newer)
      expect(person.latest_event).not_to eq(older)
    end
  end

  describe "#days_until_due" do
    let(:person) { create(:person, frequency_weeks: 2.0) }

    it "returns nil when no events" do
      expect(person.days_until_due).to be_nil
    end

    it "is negative when overdue" do
      create(:event, occurred_at: 30.days.ago, people: [person])
      expect(person.days_until_due).to be < 0
    end

    it "is positive when upcoming" do
      create(:event, occurred_at: 1.day.ago, people: [person])
      expect(person.days_until_due).to be > 0
    end
  end
end

class PeopleController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_person, only: %i[show edit update destroy]

  def index
    @people = Person.order(:name)
  end

  def show
    @events = @person.events.recent
  end

  def new
    @person = Person.new
  end

  def edit; end

  def create
    @person = Person.new(person_params)
    if @person.save
      redirect_to @person, notice: "Person was successfully created."
    else
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  def update
    if @person.update(person_params)
      redirect_to @person, notice: "Person was successfully updated."
    else
      render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  def destroy
    @person.destroy
    redirect_to people_path, notice: "Person was successfully deleted.", status: :see_other
  end

  private

  def set_person
    @person = Person.find(params[:id])
  end

  def person_params
    params.expect(
      person: [
        :name,
        :email,
        :timezone,
        :preferred_start_hour,
        :preferred_end_hour,
        :frequency_weeks,
        :notes
      ]
    )
  end
end

class EventsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_event, only: %i[show edit update destroy]

  def index
    @events = Event.recent.includes(:people)
  end

  def show; end

  def new
    @event = Event.new(occurred_at: Time.current)
  end

  def edit; end

  def create
    @event = Event.new(event_params)
    if @event.save
      redirect_to @event, notice: "Event was successfully created."
    else
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  def update
    if @event.update(event_params)
      redirect_to @event, notice: "Event was successfully updated."
    else
      render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  def destroy
    @event.destroy
    redirect_to events_path, notice: "Event was successfully deleted.", status: :see_other
  end

  private

  def set_event
    @event = Event.find(params[:id])
  end

  def event_params
    # `person_ids: []` is the ActiveRecord idiom for has_many :through assignment.
    # Blank values come in as [""] from unchecked boxes; compact them out.
    permitted = params.expect(
      event: [
        :occurred_at,
        :medium,
        :title,
        :notes,
        { person_ids: [] }
      ]
    )
    permitted[:person_ids] = Array(permitted[:person_ids]).reject(&:blank?)
    permitted
  end
end

require "rails_helper"

RSpec.describe "People", type: :request do
  let(:valid_attrs) do
    {
      name: "Alice Tester",
      email: "alice.tester@example.com",
      timezone: "America/Chicago",
      preferred_start_hour: 9,
      preferred_end_hour: 21,
      frequency_weeks: 4.0,
      notes: "met at conference"
    }
  end

  let(:invalid_attrs) do
    valid_attrs.merge(email: "nope", name: "")
  end

  describe "GET /people" do
    it "renders the index" do
      create_list(:person, 3)
      get people_path
      expect(response).to have_http_status(:ok)
      expect(response.body).to include("People")
    end
  end

  describe "GET /people/:id" do
    it "renders the show page" do
      person = create(:person)
      get person_path(person)
      expect(response).to have_http_status(:ok)
      expect(response.body).to include(person.name)
    end
  end

  describe "GET /people/new" do
    it "renders the form" do
      get new_person_path
      expect(response).to have_http_status(:ok)
    end
  end

  describe "POST /people" do
    it "creates a person with valid params" do
      expect {
        post people_path, params: { person: valid_attrs }
      }.to change(Person, :count).by(1)
      expect(response).to redirect_to(person_path(Person.last))
    end

    it "re-renders new with invalid params" do
      expect {
        post people_path, params: { person: invalid_attrs }
      }.not_to change(Person, :count)
      expect(response).to have_http_status(:unprocessable_entity)
    end
  end

  describe "GET /people/:id/edit" do
    it "renders the edit form" do
      person = create(:person)
      get edit_person_path(person)
      expect(response).to have_http_status(:ok)
    end
  end

  describe "PATCH /people/:id" do
    it "updates with valid params" do
      person = create(:person, name: "Old Name")
      patch person_path(person), params: { person: { name: "New Name" } }
      expect(response).to redirect_to(person_path(person))
      expect(person.reload.name).to eq("New Name")
    end

    it "re-renders edit with invalid params" do
      person = create(:person)
      patch person_path(person), params: { person: { email: "bad" } }
      expect(response).to have_http_status(:unprocessable_entity)
    end
  end

  describe "DELETE /people/:id" do
    it "destroys the person" do
      person = create(:person)
      expect {
        delete person_path(person)
      }.to change(Person, :count).by(-1)
      expect(response).to redirect_to(people_path)
    end
  end
end

  describe "GET /people/:id/edit" do
    it "renders the edit form" do
      person = create(:person)
      get edit_person_path(person)
      expect(response).to have_http_status(:ok)
    end
  end

  describe "PATCH /people/:id" do
    it "updates with valid params" do
      person = create(:person, name: "Old Name")
      patch person_path(person), params: { person: { name: "New Name" } }
      expect(response).to redirect_to(person_path(person))
      expect(person.reload.name).to eq("New Name")
    end

    it "re-renders edit with invalid params" do
      person = create(:person)
      patch person_path(person), params: { person: { email: "bad" } }
      expect(response).to have_http_status(:unprocessable_entity)
    end
  end

  describe "DELETE /people/:id" do
    it "destroys the person" do
      person = create(:person)
      expect {
        delete person_path(person)
      }.to change(Person, :count).by(-1)
      expect(response).to redirect_to(people_path)
    end
  end
end

require "rails_helper"

RSpec.describe "Events", type: :request do
  let(:person_a) { create(:person) }
  let(:person_b) { create(:person) }

  let(:valid_attrs) do
    {
      occurred_at: 1.day.ago,
      medium: "coffee",
      title: "Morning check-in",
      notes: "Nice time",
      person_ids: [person_a.id, person_b.id]
    }
  end

  describe "GET /events" do
    it "renders the index" do
      create(:event, people: [person_a])
      get events_path
      expect(response).to have_http_status(:ok)
      expect(response.body).to include("Events")
    end
  end

  describe "GET /events/:id" do
    it "renders the show page" do
      event = create(:event, title: "Birthday", people: [person_a])
      get event_path(event)
      expect(response).to have_http_status(:ok)
      expect(response.body).to include("Birthday")
    end
  end

  describe "POST /events" do
    it "creates an event with multiple participants" do
      expect {
        post events_path, params: { event: valid_attrs }
      }.to change(Event, :count).by(1)
      event = Event.last
      expect(event.people).to contain_exactly(person_a, person_b)
      expect(response).to redirect_to(event_path(event))
    end

    it "fails when no participants are selected" do
      expect {
        post events_path, params: { event: valid_attrs.merge(person_ids: []) }
      }.not_to change(Event, :count)
      expect(response).to have_http_status(:unprocessable_entity)
    end

    it "fails when occurred_at is in the future" do
      expect {
        post events_path, params: { event: valid_attrs.merge(occurred_at: 1.day.from_now) }
      }.not_to change(Event, :count)
      expect(response).to have_http_status(:unprocessable_entity)
    end
  end

  describe "PATCH /events/:id" do
    it "updates attributes and participants" do
      event = create(:event, title: "Old", people: [person_a])
      person_c = create(:person)

      patch event_path(event), params: {
        event: { title: "New", person_ids: [person_a.id, person_c.id] }
      }

      expect(response).to redirect_to(event_path(event))
      event.reload
      expect(event.title).to eq("New")
      expect(event.people).to contain_exactly(person_a, person_c)
    end
  end

  describe "DELETE /events/:id" do
    it "destroys the event and its join rows" do
      event = create(:event, people: [person_a, person_b])
      expect {
        delete event_path(event)
      }.to change(Event, :count).by(-1)
        .and change(EventParticipant, :count).by(-2)
      expect(response).to redirect_to(events_path)
    end
  end
end

input.content:
module ApplicationHelper
  # Map Rails flash types to Bootstrap contextual classes.
  def flash_bootstrap_class(flash_type)
    case flash_type.to_s
    when "notice", "success" then "success"
    when "alert", "error"    then "danger"
    when "warning"           then "warning"
    else                          "info"
    end
  end

  # Bootstrap badge color for an Event medium.
  def medium_badge_class(medium)
    case medium
    when "call"      then "bg-primary"
    when "video"     then "bg-info text-dark"
    when "coffee"    then "bg-warning text-dark"
    when "text"      then "bg-secondary"
    when "in_person" then "bg-success"
    else                   "bg-dark"
    end
  end

  # Present a "days until due" number in words with a matching Bootstrap badge.
  # Returns `[label, css_class]` so callers can render however they like.
  def days_until_due_badge(days)
    return ["No events yet", "bg-secondary"] if days.nil?
    return ["Due today", "bg-warning text-dark"] if days.zero?
    return ["#{days.abs} days overdue", "bg-danger"] if days.negative?

    ["Due in #{days} days", "bg-success"]
  end
end

class PeopleController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_person, only: %i[show edit update destroy]

  def index
    @people = Person.order(:name)
  end

  def show
    @events = @person.events.recent
  end

  def new
    @person = Person.new
  end

  def edit; end

  def create
    @person = Person.new(person_params)
    if @person.save
      redirect_to @person, notice: "Person was successfully created."
    else
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  def update
    if @person.update(person_params)
      redirect_to @person, notice: "Person was successfully updated."
    else
      render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  def destroy
    @person.destroy
    redirect_to people_path, notice: "Person was successfully deleted.", status: :see_other
  end

  private

  def set_person
    @person = Person.find(params[:id])
  end

  def person_params
    params.require(:person).permit(
      :name,
      :email,
      :timezone,
      :preferred_start_hour,
      :preferred_end_hour,
      :frequency_weeks,
      :notes
    )
  end
end
